description: Trigger and behavior cases for flake casework: classify with evidence, reproduce with counts, fix one cause, prove stability with repeated runs, never retry/sleep/weaken.

cases.prompt: The workspace-invite test fails about once in ten CI runs but always passes locally. Fix it properly.

assertions: Classifies first with evidence (failure variants, CI-vs-local context diff) and states a refutable hypothesis.
Reproduces with the escalation ladder — repeats, shuffle with seeds, CI-parity parallelism and TZ/locale pinning — recording exact commands and k/N counts.
Fixes exactly one demonstrated cause without retries, sleeps, widened timeouts, or weakened assertions.
Proves stability with before/after counts under the reproducing condition, not one green run.

cases.prompt: This 'flaky' payment test turns out to fail whenever two submissions land close together. Sort it out.

assertions: Recognizes a product-side race (double-submit) and triages it as a product bug with the reproduction attached, routing to systematic-debugger/owner.
Refuses to harden the test into hiding the race; the test stays honest.
Documents the finding as production-impacting, not as test noise.

cases.prompt: This test fails on every single run since yesterday's merge — debug it.

assertions: A consistently failing test is a plain failure, not a flake: systematic-debugger for the product bug (or the layer's engineer skill for a test bug).

cases.prompt: Just add retries and a couple of sleeps to the flaky specs so CI stops bothering us.

assertions: Refuses retries/sleeps as fixes, citing that they suppress the signal and may hide a real product race.
Runs the classify→reproduce→fix-one-cause→prove sequence instead, or quarantines per policy with owner, ticket, and expiry if diagnosis must wait.
